source: Through the dismal prison twilight, his new charge accompanied him by corridor and staircase, many doors clanging and locking behind them, until they came into a large, low, vaulted chamber, crowded with prisoners of both sexes. source: His removal, to make way for other accused persons who were to be tried, rescued him from these caresses for the moment. Five were to be tried together, next, as enemies of the Republic, forasmuch as they had not assisted it by word or deed. So quick was the Tribunal to compensate itself and the nation for a chance lost, that these five came down to him before he left the place, condemned to die within twenty-four hours. The first of them told him so, with the customary prison sign of Death—a raised finger—and they all added in words, “Long live the Republic!”
domestication: 他被移走，好让其他被告上庭受审，这暂时让他摆脱了这些“亲热”。接下来，有五个人要一起受审，因为他们没有以言行支持共和国，被视为共和国的敌人。法庭为了弥补错失的机会，迅速作出判决，这五个人在他离开前就被定罪，要在二十四小时内处死。第一个人用狱中惯用的死刑手势——举起手指——告诉他这个消息，其他人都附和道：“共和国万岁！”
foreignization: 他的移除，为了让其他要受审的人有位置，暂时让他从这些拥抱中解脱出来。接下来，五个人将一起作为共和国的敌人受审，因为他们没有以言语或行动支持共和国。法庭如此迅速地补偿自己和国家失去的机会，以至于这五人在他离开这个地方之前就来找他，被判在二十四小时内处死。其中第一个人用惯常的监狱死亡手势——举起的手指——告诉他这一点，并且他们所有人都用言语补充道，“共和国万岁！”

source: With drooping heads and tremulous tails, they mashed their way through the thick mud, floundering and stumbling between whiles, as if they were falling to pieces at the larger joints. As often as the driver rested them and brought them to a stand, with a wary “Wo-ho! so-ho-then!” the near leader violently shook his head and everything upon it—like an unusually emphatic horse, denying that the coach could be got up the hill. Whenever the leader made this rattle, the passenger started, as a nervous passenger might, and was disturbed in mind.
domestication: 它们低着头，拖着发抖的尾巴，在厚厚的泥泞中艰难前行，时不时地跌跌撞撞，好像关节都要散架了。每当车夫停下让它们休息，小心翼翼地吆喝一声“吁——好嘞！”靠近前面的马就会猛地摇晃脑袋，上面的东西也跟着乱颤，仿佛一匹特别倔强的马，坚决表示马车不可能爬上去。每回这马一摇头，乘客就跟着紧张起来，心里七上八下的。
foreignization: 他们低着头，尾巴颤抖，踏着厚厚的泥浆前行，不时跌跌撞撞，仿佛它们的大关节都要散架了。每当车夫让它们休息，用警惕的“哦-哦！就这样-然后！”让它们停下时，领头的马就会猛烈地摇晃它的头和上面的一切——就像一匹特别强调的马，否认马车能爬上山坡。每当领头马发出这种响动时，乘客就会像一个神经质的乘客那样惊跳起来，并感到心神不安。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

With drooping heads and tremulous tails, they mashed their way through the thick mud, floundering and stumbling between whiles, as if they were falling to pieces at the larger joints. As often as the driver rested them and brought them to a stand, with a wary “Wo-ho! so-ho-then!” the near leader violently shook his head and everything upon it—like an unusually emphatic horse, denying that the coach could be got up the hill. Whenever the leader made this rattle, the passenger started, as a nervous passenger might, and was disturbed in mind.
它们低着头，拖着发抖的尾巴，在厚厚的泥泞中艰难前行，时不时地跌跌撞撞，好像关节都要散架了。每当车夫停下让它们休息，小心翼翼地吆喝一声“吁——好嘞！”靠近前面的马就会猛地摇晃脑袋，上面的东西也跟着乱颤，仿佛一匹特别倔强的马，坚决表示马车不可能爬上去。每回这马一摇头，乘客就跟着紧张起来，心里七上八下的。

source: “My dear Manette, it is the case of an old and a prolonged shock, of great acuteness and severity to the affections, the feelings, the—the—as you express it—the mind. The mind. It is the case of a shock under which the sufferer was borne down, one cannot say for how long, because I believe he cannot calculate the time himself, and there are no other means of getting at it. It is the case of a shock from which the sufferer recovered, by a process that he cannot trace himself—as I once heard him publicly relate in a striking manner. It is the case of a shock from which he has recovered, so completely, as to be a highly intelligent man, capable of close application of mind, and great exertion of body, and of constantly making fresh additions to his stock of knowledge, which was already very large. But, unfortunately, there has been,” he paused and took a deep breath—“a slight relapse.”
domestication: 亲爱的曼内特，这是一桩旧疾，拖了很长时间，对情感、心灵——用你的说法就是精神——造成了极大的刺激和伤害。这病让患者不堪重负，持续了多久无人知晓，因为他自己也记不清时间，更没有其他方法可以得知。病情一度好转，他自己都描述不出是如何逐渐恢复的，我曾经听他公开讲述过这个过程，非常动人。他恢复得很好，成了一个聪明绝顶的人，既有专注力，又有体力，还能不断学习新知，知识渊博。然而，不幸的是，”他顿了顿，深吸了一口气——“最近又有点复发了。”
foreignization: “我亲爱的马内特，这是一个长期的、严重的、对情感、感觉、你所说的——心灵——造成的强烈冲击的病例。心灵。这是一个患者在其中被压垮的冲击，持续了多久无法确定，因为他自己也无法计算时间，也没有其他方法可以得知。这是一个患者从中恢复过来的冲击，他自己也无法追踪恢复的过程——我曾听他公开讲述过这一过程，非常动人。这是一个他已经完全恢复的冲击，他现在是一个非常聪明的人，能够集中精力，身体力行，不断充实自己已经很丰富的知识库。但是，不幸的是，”他停顿了一下，深吸了一口气——“有轻微的复发。”

domestication: 不过，还有其他的声音，从远处传来，在这段时间里一直阴沉沉地在角落里回荡。直到小露西六岁生日前后，这些声音开始变得可怕起来，就像是法国刮起了大风暴，海浪汹涌澎湃，让人不寒而栗。

source: It was now Young Jerry’s turn to approach the gate: which he did, holding his breath. Crouching down again in a corner there, and looking in, he made out the three fishermen creeping through some rank grass! and all the gravestones in the churchyard—it was a large churchyard that they were in—looking on like ghosts in white, while the church tower itself looked on like the ghost of a monstrous giant. They did not creep far, before they stopped and stood upright. And then they began to fish.
domestication: 轮到小杰里上场了，他屏住呼吸，小心翼翼地靠近大门。他蹲在角落里，往里窥探，只见那三个渔夫正偷偷摸摸地穿过一片茂密的杂草！墓地里的每一座墓碑——这可是一座大墓地——都像白茫茫的鬼魂一样盯着他们，而教堂的钟楼则像一个巨大怪物的幽灵，静静地注视着这一切。他们没走多远就停下了，站直了身子，然后开始钓鱼。
foreignization: 现在轮到小杰里走近大门了：他屏住呼吸这么做了。再次蹲在一个角落里，往里看，他看到三个渔夫正穿过一些茂密的草！而教堂墓地里的所有墓碑——他们所在的是一片很大的墓地——像穿着白衣服的鬼魂一样看着，而教堂塔楼本身则像一个巨大怪物的鬼魂一样注视着。他们没有爬多远，就停下来站直了。然后他们开始钓鱼。

It was now Young Jerry’s turn to approach the gate: which he did, holding his breath. Crouching down again in a corner there, and looking in, he made out the three fishermen creeping through some rank grass! and all the gravestones in the churchyard—it was a large churchyard that they were in—looking on like ghosts in white, while the church tower itself looked on like the ghost of a monstrous giant. They did not creep far, before they stopped and stood upright. And then they began to fish.
现在轮到小杰里走近大门了：他屏住呼吸这么做了。再次蹲在一个角落里，往里看，他看到三个渔夫正穿过一些茂密的草！而教堂墓地里的所有墓碑——他们所在的是一片很大的墓地——像穿着白衣服的鬼魂一样看着，而教堂塔楼本身则像一个巨大怪物的鬼魂一样注视着。他们没有爬多远，就停下来站直了。然后他们开始钓鱼。

source: The great door clanged behind him, and Monsieur the Marquis crossed a hall grim with certain old boar-spears, swords, and knives of the chase; grimmer with certain heavy riding-rods and riding-whips, of which many a peasant, gone to his benefactor Death, had felt the weight when his lord was angry.

source: The messenger rode back at an easy trot, stopping pretty often at ale-houses by the way to drink, but evincing a tendency to keep his own counsel, and to keep his hat cocked over his eyes. He had eyes that assorted very well with that decoration, being of a surface black, with no depth in the colour or form, and much too near together—as if they were afraid of being found out in something, singly, if they kept too far apart. They had a sinister expression, under an old cocked-hat like a three-cornered spittoon, and over a great muffler for the chin and throat, which descended nearly to the wearer’s knees. When he stopped for drink, he moved this muffler with his left hand, only while he poured his liquor in with his right; as soon as that was done, he muffled again.
domestication: 信使慢悠悠地骑马回程，路上常在酒馆停下喝酒，但他总是守口如瓶，帽檐也压得低低的，遮住眼睛。他的眼睛与这顶帽子十分相配，黑得浅薄，没有深邃感，而且靠得太近，好像彼此害怕被单独揭穿什么秘密似的。在他那顶像三角痰盂的老式歪帽子下，这双眼透着一股邪气，再加上一条几乎垂到膝盖的大围巾，把下巴和脖子都严严实实地裹住了。每到酒馆喝酒时，他只用左手掀起围巾一角，右手倒酒，喝完立刻又裹紧了。
foreignization: 信使慢悠悠地策马而回，途中经常在路边的小酒馆停下来喝酒，但他表现出一种保守秘密的倾向，帽子总是斜戴在眼睛上。他那双眼睛与这种装饰非常相称，表面漆黑一片，颜色和形态都没有深度，而且靠得太近——仿佛它们如果离得太远，就会单独被发现有什么不可告人的秘密。在这顶像三角形痰盂的老式帽子下，这双眼睛显得格外阴险，而那条几乎垂到膝盖的大围巾遮住了下巴和喉咙。当他停下来喝酒时，只用左手移动围巾，右手倒酒；一旦喝完，他又再次把脸捂了起来。

When he stopped for drink, he moved this muffler with his left hand, only while he poured his liquor in with his right; as soon as that was done, he muffled again.

source: “But I do, and you must take my word for it. Well! If you could endure to have such a worthless fellow, and a fellow of such indifferent reputation, coming and going at odd times, I should ask that I might be permitted to come and go as a privileged person here; that I might be regarded as an useless (and I would add, if it were not for the resemblance I detected between you and me, an unornamental) piece of furniture, tolerated for its old service, and taken no notice of. I doubt if I should abuse the permission. It is a hundred to one if I should avail myself of it four times in a year. It would satisfy me, I dare say, to know that I had it.”

source: This dialogue had taken place at Mr. Lorry’s usual desk, with Monseigneur swarming within a yard or two of it, boastful of what he would do to avenge himself on the rascal-people before long. It was too much the way of Monseigneur under his reverses as a refugee, and it was much too much the way of native British orthodoxy, to talk of this terrible Revolution as if it were the only harvest ever known under the skies that had not been sown—as if nothing had ever been done, or omitted to be done, that had led to it—as if observers of the wretched millions in France, and of the misused and perverted resources that should have made them prosperous, had not seen it inevitably coming, years before, and had not in plain words recorded what they saw. Such vapouring, combined with the extravagant plots of Monseigneur for the restoration of a state of things that had utterly exhausted itself, and worn out Heaven and earth as well as itself, was hard to be endured without some remonstrance by any sane man who knew the truth. And it was such vapouring all about his ears, like a troublesome confusion of blood in his own head, added to a latent uneasiness in his mind, which had already made Charles Darnay restless, and which still kept him so.
domestication: 这场对话发生在老洛里先生的办公桌旁，而蒙塞尼尔大人则在一旁嚣张地夸口，说他不久后定要如何报复那帮贱民。流亡中的蒙塞尼尔大人失势后的这副嘴脸，英国本土那些正统人士也是一样，总爱把这场可怕的革命说得好像天上掉下来的一样，仿佛它无端发生，又似乎没有任何作为或不作为导致了它的爆发——就好比有人早年就看出了法国数百万贫苦大众的悲惨境遇和被滥用、扭曲的资源本应让他们繁荣昌盛，并且早已直截了当地记录下所见所闻，预言这一切终将到来。这种胡言乱语，再加上蒙塞尼尔大人那些荒谬绝伦的复辟计划，对于任何了解真相的理智之人来说，实在是难以忍受。这种声音像脑中的混乱血流一样，让查尔斯·达内心神不宁，原本就有些焦虑不安的他，现在更是坐立难安。
foreignization: 这段对话是在洛里先生的惯常办公桌前进行的，与此同时，一位贵族在距此一两码的地方嗡嗡作响，夸耀他不久将如何报复这些流氓百姓。流亡中的贵族在遭受挫折时，以及英国本土正统派，在谈到这场可怕的革命时，总是太过倾向于将其描述为唯一未曾播种便收获的果实——仿佛从未有过任何行为或疏忽导致了它的发生——仿佛观察家们没有在多年前就预见到了法国数百万民众的悲惨境况和被滥用、扭曲的资源本应使他们繁荣起来的局面，并且没有用直白的语言记录下他们所看到的一切。这种空谈，加上贵族们为了恢复一种已经彻底耗尽自身并连同天地一起疲惫不堪的状态而制定的荒谬计划，对于任何了解真相的理智之人来说，都难以忍受而不提出抗议。这种围绕着他耳边的空谈，就像他自己头脑中令人烦恼的血液混乱一样，加上心中潜藏的不安，已经让查尔斯·达内坐立不安，并且仍然让他如此。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

This dialogue had taken place at Mr. Lorry’s usual desk, with Monseigneur swarming within a yard or two of it, boastful of what he would do to avenge himself on the rascal-people before long. It was too much the way of Monseigneur under his reverses as a refugee, and it was much too much the way of native British orthodoxy, to talk of this terrible Revolution as if it were the only harvest ever known under the skies that had not been sown—as if nothing had ever been done, or omitted to be done, that had led to it—as if observers of the wretched millions in France, and of the misused and perverted resources that should have made them prosperous, had not seen it inevitably coming, years before, and had not in plain words recorded what they saw. Such vapouring, combined with the extravagant plots of Monseigneur for the restoration of a state of things that had utterly exhausted itself, and worn out Heaven and earth as well as itself, was hard to be endured without some remonstrance by any sane man who knew the truth. And it was such vapouring all about his ears, like a troublesome confusion of blood in his own head, added to a latent uneasiness in his mind, which had already made Charles Darnay restless, and which still kept him so.

这场对话发生在老洛里先生的办公桌旁，而蒙塞尼尔大人则在一旁嚣张地夸口，说他不久后定要如何报复那帮贱民。流亡中的蒙塞尼尔大人失势后的这副嘴脸，英国本土那些正统人士也是一样，总爱把这场可怕的革命说得好像天上掉下来的一样，仿佛它无端发生，又似乎没有任何作为或不作为导致了它的爆发——就好比有人早年就看出了法国数百万贫苦大众的悲惨境遇和被滥用、扭曲的资源本应让他们繁荣昌盛，并且早已直截了当地记录下所见所闻，预言这一切终将到来。这种胡言乱语，再加上蒙塞尼尔大人那些荒谬绝伦的复辟计划，对于任何了解真相的理智之人来说，实在是难以忍受。这种声音像脑中的混乱血流一样，让查尔斯·达内心神不宁，原本就有些焦虑不安的他，现在更是坐立难安。

This dialogue had taken place at Mr. Lorry’s usual desk, with Monseigneur swarming within a yard or two of it, boastful of what he would do to avenge himself on the rascal-people before long. It was too much the way of Monseigneur under his reverses as a refugee, and it was much too much the way of native British orthodoxy, to talk of this terrible Revolution as if it were the only harvest ever known under the skies that had not been sown—as if nothing had ever been done, or omitted to be done, that had led to it—as if observers of the wretched millions in France, and of the misused and perverted resources that should have made them prosperous, had not seen it inevitably coming, years before, and had not in plain words recorded what they saw. Such vapouring, combined with the extravagant plots of Monseigneur for the restoration of a state of things that had utterly exhausted itself, and worn out Heaven and earth as well as itself, was hard to be endured without some remonstrance by any sane man who knew the truth. And it was such vapouring all about his ears, like a troublesome confusion of blood in his own head, added to a latent uneasiness in his mind, which had already made Charles Darnay restless, and which still kept him so.

这段对话是在洛里先生的惯常办公桌前进行的，与此同时，一位贵族在距此一两码的地方嗡嗡作响，夸耀他不久将如何报复这些流氓百姓。流亡中的贵族在遭受挫折时，以及英国本土正统派，在谈到这场可怕的革命时，总是太过倾向于将其描述为唯一未曾播种便收获的果实——仿佛从未有过任何行为或疏忽导致了它的发生——仿佛观察家们没有在多年前就预见到了法国数百万民众的悲惨境况和被滥用、扭曲的资源本应使他们繁荣起来的局面，并且没有用直白的语言记录下他们所看到的一切。这种空谈，加上贵族们为了恢复一种已经彻底耗尽自身并连同天地一起疲惫不堪的状态而制定的荒谬计划，对于任何了解真相的理智之人来说，都难以忍受而不提出抗议。这种围绕着他耳边的空谈，就像他自己头脑中令人烦恼的血液混乱一样，加上心中潜藏的不安，已经让查尔斯·达内坐立不安，并且仍然让他如此。

source: Monseigneur, as a class, had dissociated himself from the phenomenon of his not being appreciated: of his being so little wanted in France, as to incur considerable danger of receiving his dismissal from it, and this life together. Like the fabled rustic who raised the Devil with infinite pains, and was so terrified at the sight of him that he could ask the Enemy no question, but immediately fled; so, Monseigneur, after boldly reading the Lord’s Prayer backwards for a great number of years, and performing many other potent spells for compelling the Evil One, no sooner beheld him in his terrors than he took to his noble heels.
domestication: 老爷们作为一个阶层，已经和他们不受待见的现象脱钩了：在法国，他们如此不受欢迎，以至于面临着被赶出法国甚至被赶出人世的危险。这就像那个传说中的乡下人，费尽千辛万苦召唤出了魔鬼，结果一见到魔鬼就吓得魂不附体，连个问题都没敢问就撒腿跑了。同样，老爷们多年来大胆地倒念主祷文，还施了许多强大的咒语来迫使邪魔现身，可一旦真的在恐惧中看到了邪魔，立刻就拔腿逃之夭夭。

source: They travelled in the night, halting an hour or two after daybreak, and lying by until the twilight fell. The escort were so wretchedly clothed, that they twisted straw round their bare legs, and thatched their ragged shoulders to keep the wet off. Apart from the personal discomfort of being so attended, and apart from such considerations of present danger as arose from one of the patriots being chronically drunk, and carrying his musket very recklessly, Charles Darnay did not allow the restraint that was laid upon him to awaken any serious fears in his breast; for, he reasoned with himself that it could have no reference to the merits of an individual case that was not yet stated, and of representations, confirmable by the prisoner in the Abbaye, that were not yet made.
domestication: 他们夜间赶路，天亮后休息一两个小时，等到黄昏再继续前行。护送的人衣衫褴褛，只得用稻草缠绕在裸露的腿上，又用稻草覆盖在破烂的肩膀上，以挡风雨。除了这样的陪同让人心生不适，再加上其中一位爱国者常年酗酒，枪支随意乱拿，带来了一些眼前的危险之外，查尔斯·达尔内并没有因为身上的束缚而感到真正的恐惧；他心里盘算着，这不可能是针对他个人情况的措施，毕竟他的案子还没陈述，也没能在阿拜监狱里证实他的请求。
foreignization: 他们在夜间旅行，在天亮后一两个小时停歇，直到暮色降临时才再次出发。护送人员衣衫褴褛，以至于他们把稻草缠绕在裸露的腿上，并在破烂的肩膀上覆盖稻草以挡雨水。除了这种随行方式给他个人带来的不适，以及当前由于一位爱国者长期醉酒并随意携带火枪而产生的危险之外，查尔斯·达内并不让加诸于他的限制引起自己内心的严重担忧；因为他自认为，这种限制与尚未陈述的个人案件的优劣无关，也与尚待由阿拜监狱中的囚犯证实的情况无关。

source: He left escort and escorted without saying a word, however, and went into the guard-room; meanwhile, they sat upon their horses outside the gate. Looking about him while in this state of suspense, Charles Darnay observed that the gate was held by a mixed guard of soldiers and patriots, the latter far outnumbering the former; and that while ingress into the city for peasants’ carts bringing in supplies, and for similar traffic and traffickers, was easy enough, egress, even for the homeliest people, was very difficult. A numerous medley of men and women, not to mention beasts and vehicles of various sorts, was waiting to issue forth; but, the previous identification was so strict, that they filtered through the barrier very slowly. Some of these people knew their turn for examination to be so far off, that they lay down on the ground to sleep or smoke, while others talked together, or loitered about. The red cap and tri-colour cockade were universal, both among men and women.
domestication: 他一句话没说就离开了护送队伍，径直走进了警卫室；而他们则骑着马守在门外。查尔斯·达尔内心里忐忑不安，环顾四周，发现城门由士兵和爱国者组成的混合岗哨守卫着，其中爱国者比士兵多得多。农民的供应车进城还比较容易，但即使是普通人，想要出城也非常困难。许多男女老少，还有各种牲畜和车辆，都在等着出城，但由于身份查验极其严格，通过速度非常缓慢。有些人的检查顺序排得特别靠后，索性躺在地上睡觉或抽烟，有的人在聊天，有的四处闲逛。红帽子和三色徽章几乎是人手一个，男女皆是如此。
foreignization: 他一句话也没说就离开了护送人员，自己护送自己，走进了哨所；与此同时，他们骑在马上守在门外。查尔斯·达奈在这种悬而未决的状态下环顾四周，注意到大门由士兵和爱国者组成的混合卫队把守，后者远远多于前者；而对于农民的马车带入补给以及类似的交易和商贩来说，进城很容易，但即使是相貌平平的人出城也非常困难。许多男女混杂在一起，更不用说各种各样的牲畜和车辆，都在等待外出；但由于之前的身份验证非常严格，他们通过关卡的速度非常缓慢。有些人知道自己的检查顺序还很靠后，便躺在地上睡觉或抽烟，而其他人则互相交谈或四处闲逛。红帽子和三色帽徽在男女中都很普遍。

source: “In making some alterations, the workmen came upon an old dungeon, which had been, for many years, built up and forgotten. Every stone of its inner wall was covered by inscriptions which had been carved by prisoners—dates, names, complaints, and prayers. Upon a corner stone in an angle of the wall, one prisoner, who seemed to have gone to execution, had cut as his last work, three letters. They were done with some very poor instrument, and hurriedly, with an unsteady hand. At first, they were read as D. I. C.; but, on being more carefully examined, the last letter was found to be G. There was no record or legend of any prisoner with those initials, and many fruitless guesses were made what the name could have been. At length, it was suggested that the letters were not initials, but the complete word, DIG. The floor was examined very carefully under the inscription, and, in the earth beneath a stone, or tile, or some fragment of paving, were found the ashes of a paper, mingled with the ashes of a small leathern case or bag. What the unknown prisoner had written will never be read, but he had written something, and hidden it away to keep it from the gaoler.”
domestication: 工匠们在改造时发现了一个早已被人遗忘的旧地牢。这地牢很多年前就被封起来了。地牢内墙上的每一块石头都刻满了囚犯的铭文，有日期、名字、抱怨和祈祷。在一个墙角的石头上，一个似乎即将被处决的囚犯留下了他最后的作品——三个字母。这些字母是用极简陋的工具匆忙刻下的，手还抖个不停。一开始，人们读到的是D.I.C.；但仔细检查后，发现最后一个字母其实是G。没有关于这个囚犯的任何记录或传说，大家猜了好一阵子，都不知这是谁的名字。后来，有人提议说这三个字母并不是名字的首字母，而是一个完整的单词——DIG。于是，人们仔细检查了刻字下面的地面，在一块石头或瓦片下面的土里找到了一张纸的灰烬，还有一些小皮袋的残余。这位不知名的囚犯写了些什么，永远没人能知道了，但他确实写过东西，并藏了起来，不让狱卒发现。
foreignization: 在进行一些改造时，工人们发现了一个古老的地牢，这个地牢多年来已经被封堵并遗忘了。地牢的内墙上的每一块石头都刻有囚犯们雕刻的铭文——日期、名字、抱怨和祈祷。在墙角的一块角石上，一个似乎已被处决的囚犯刻下了他的最后作品，三个字母。这些字母是用非常简陋的工具匆忙刻下的，手也不稳。起初，它们被读作D. I. C.；但经过更仔细的检查后，发现最后一个字母是G。没有关于有这些首字母的囚犯的记录或传说，许多人猜测这可能是哪个名字的首字母。最终，有人建议这些字母不是首字母，而是完整的单词DIG。地板在铭文下方被仔细检查，发现在一块石头、瓷砖或某些铺地碎片下面，有一张纸的灰烬与一个小皮革袋或袋子的灰烬混在一起。这位不知名的囚犯写的内容将永远不会被读到，但他确实写了些什么，并将其藏起来以避免狱卒发现。

source: “To the first, then. He is of a studious habit, and unusually energetic; he applies himself with great ardour to the acquisition of professional knowledge, to the conducting of experiments, to many things. Now, does he do too much?”
domestication: 先说第一个吧。他好学又格外有干劲，对专业知识的钻研、实验操作等各种事情都充满热情。可问题是，他是不是做得太多了？
foreignization: “那么，先说第一个。他习惯于学习，并且异常有活力；他以极大的热情致力于专业知知识的获取、实验的进行、很多事情。现在，他做得太多了吗？”

source: He moved from end to end of his voluptuous bedroom, looking again at the scraps of the day’s journey that came unbidden into his mind; the slow toil up the hill at sunset, the setting sun, the descent, the mill, the prison on the crag, the little village in the hollow, the peasants at the fountain, and the mender of roads with his blue cap pointing out the chain under the carriage. That fountain suggested the Paris fountain, the little bundle lying on the step, the women bending over it, and the tall man with his arms up, crying, “Dead!”
domestication: 他从这头走到那头，在豪华的卧室里来回踱步，脑海中不由自主地浮现出今日旅途中的片片段段：夕阳下的缓慢攀爬，落日余晖，下山的路，磨坊，峭壁上的监狱，山谷里的小村庄，水泉边的农民，还有那个戴蓝帽子的修路人，指着车下的锁链。那口泉水让他想起了巴黎的喷泉，石阶上躺着的小包裹，弯腰查看的女人，还有那个高个子男人，双臂高举，喊着：“死了！”

source: He knew very well, that in his horror of the deed which had culminated the bad deeds and bad reputation of the old family house, in his resentful suspicions of his uncle, and in the aversion with which his conscience regarded the crumbling fabric that he was supposed to uphold, he had acted imperfectly. He knew very well, that in his love for Lucie, his renunciation of his social place, though by no means new to his own mind, had been hurried and incomplete. He knew that he ought to have systematically worked it out and supervised it, and that he had meant to do it, and that it had never been done.
domestication: 他心里明白，对于老家族那桩最终导致声名狼藉的罪行，他感到十分恐惧；对叔叔心存猜忌；对良心上反感的那座摇摇欲坠的老宅，本该尽职维护，却做得不够好。他也知道，为了露西，他放弃了自己的社会地位，虽然这念头在他心里早已有之，但行动却过于仓促，不够彻底。他本应系统地规划和监督这一切，也确实有过这样的打算，但最终还是没能付诸实施。
foreignization: 他非常清楚，在他对自己家族老宅最终的恶行和恶名感到恐惧、对叔叔心怀怨恨的猜疑，以及对良心所反对的、他本应维护的摇摇欲坠的旧体制的厌恶中，他的行为是不完美的。他非常清楚，出于对露西的爱，他放弃了自己在社会中的地位，虽然这并不是他首次有这样的想法，但这一决定却是仓促且不完整的。他知道，他本应该有系统地规划和监督这一过程，他也曾打算这样做，但最终却从未实现。

source: It was the best of times, it was the worst of times, it was the age of wisdom, it was the age of foolishness, it was the epoch of belief, it was the epoch of incredulity, it was the season of Light, it was the season of Darkness, it was the spring of hope, it was the winter of despair, we had everything before us, we had nothing before us, we were all going direct to Heaven, we were all going direct the other way—in short, the period was so far like the present period, that some of its noisiest authorities insisted on its being received, for good or for evil, in the superlative degree of comparison only.
domestication: 那是最好的时代，也是最坏的时代；是智慧的年代，也是愚昧的年代；是信仰的时期，也是怀疑的时期；是光明的季节，也是黑暗的季节；是充满希望的春天，也是绝望的冬天；我们面前什么都有，我们面前又一无所有；我们全都直奔天堂而去，我们又全都在朝相反的方向前进——简而言之，那个时代和现在如此相似，以至于当时那些最吵闹的权威人士坚持认为，对于那个时代的一切，要么好到极致，要么坏到极点。

source: Over the prisoner’s head there was a mirror, to throw the light down upon him. Crowds of the wicked and the wretched had been reflected in it, and had passed from its surface and this earth’s together. Haunted in a most ghastly manner that abominable place would have been, if the glass could ever have rendered back its reflections, as the ocean is one day to give up its dead. Some passing thought of the infamy and disgrace for which it had been reserved, may have struck the prisoner’s mind. Be that as it may, a change in his position making him conscious of a bar of light across his face, he looked up; and when he saw the glass his face flushed, and his right hand pushed the herbs away.
domestication: 囚犯头顶上挂着一面镜子，用来把光线反射到他身上。这面镜子里映过无数的恶人和可怜虫，他们从镜中消失，也从人间消失了。要是这镜子能像大海有一天会吐出沉没其中的尸体那样，把曾经映在它上面的影像都还回来，那这个地方恐怕早就成了最可怕的鬼地方了。也许囚犯想到了自己留在这镜子中的耻辱与羞愧，也可能没有，但当他变换姿势，突然意识到脸上有一道光时，抬头看见了那面镜子，脸一下子红了，右手也把草药推开了。
foreignization: 在囚犯的头顶上有一面镜子，用来将光线投射到他身上。成群的恶人和不幸者曾在这面镜子里被反映出来，并且从镜面和这个地球一同消失。如果这面镜子能够像大海有一天会交出其死者那样，将其反射出来的影像重新呈现，那这个可憎的地方将会以一种最可怕的鬼魅方式受到侵扰。对于这面镜子所保留的耻辱和羞辱，囚犯心中可能掠过一些短暂的念头。无论情况如何，由于位置的变化使他意识到脸上有一道光带，他抬头看了看；当他看到镜子时，脸红了，右手把草药推开。

source: Mr. Lorry was so exceedingly disconcerted by a question so hard to answer, that he could only look on, at a distance, with much feebler sympathy and humility, while the strong woman, having banished the inn servants under the mysterious penalty of “letting them know” something not mentioned if they stayed there, staring, recovered her charge by a regular series of gradations, and coaxed her to lay her drooping head upon her shoulder.
domestication: 洛里先生被这个难以回答的问题弄得十分尴尬，只能远远地站着，同情和谦卑都减弱了许多。那位坚强的女人则将旅店的仆人们以“让他们知道一些不该知道的事”为威胁赶走后，用一系列渐进的方式安抚她，最后哄得她把低垂的头靠在了她的肩膀上。
foreignization: 洛里先生被这样一个难以回答的问题弄得如此局促不安，以至于他只能远远地站在一旁，带着更加微弱的同情和谦卑，而那位强壮的女人则在以一种神秘的惩罚“让他们知道”某些未提及的事情为由将客栈的服务员赶走后，通过一系列逐渐升级的步骤恢复了对她的掌控，并哄她把垂下的头靠在她的肩膀上。

source: They were not agreed, and wished to retire. My Lord (perhaps with George Washington on his mind) showed some surprise that they were not agreed, but signified his pleasure that they should retire under watch and ward, and retired himself. The trial had lasted all day, and the lamps in the court were now being lighted. It began to be rumoured that the jury would be out a long while. The spectators dropped off to get refreshment, and the prisoner withdrew to the back of the dock, and sat down.
domestication: 他们意见不合，想要退庭。大人（可能心里想着乔治·华盛顿）对他们的不一致表示惊讶，但还是示意让他们在看守下退庭，自己也离开了。审讯从早到晚，法庭里的灯现在都点上了。开始有传言说陪审团要商量很久。观众们纷纷散去，去吃点东西，被告则退到被告席后面，坐了下来。
foreignization: 他们不同意，并希望退场。我的大人（可能想到了乔治·华盛顿）对他们意见不一致表示惊讶，但表示希望他们在监视下退场，然后他自己也退场了。审判持续了一整天，法庭里的灯现在开始点亮。开始有传言说陪审团将需要很长时间才能作出裁决。观众们纷纷离开去补充能量，而被告则退到被告席后面，坐了下来。

source: He recovered himself almost instantly. Rain was really falling in large drops, and he showed the back of his hand with rain-drops on it. But, he said not a single word in reference to the discovery that had been told of, and, as they went into the house, the business eye of Mr. Lorry either detected, or fancied it detected, on his face, as it turned towards Charles Darnay, the same singular look that had been upon it when it turned towards him in the passages of the Court House.
domestication: 他几乎是立刻恢复了镇定。雨确实下得很大，他还举起手背给大家看上面的雨珠。可是，对于刚才提到的那个发现，他一个字都没提。进屋的时候，洛里先生敏锐的商业眼光似乎在他脸上捕捉到了某种奇异的眼神，就像在法院走廊里转向查尔斯·达尔内时那样。
foreignization: 他几乎立刻就恢复了镇定。雨确实下得很大，他还展示了手背上沾着的雨滴。但是，对于那个已经被告知的发现，他一个字也没有提。当他们走进房子时，洛里先生那敏锐的商务眼光要么是发现了，要么是自认为发现了，在他的脸上，当他转向查尔斯·达奈时，有着与他在法院走廊中转向自己时相同的奇异表情。

source: Nothing but the production of the afflicted Gabelle’s letter from his prison of the Abbaye would have got him on so far. source: He would not have stopped then, for anything less necessary than breath, it being a spectral sort of race that he ran, and one highly desirable to get to the end of. He had a strong idea that the coffin he had seen was running after him; and, pictured as hopping on behind him, bolt upright, upon its narrow end, always on the point of overtaking him and hopping on at his side—perhaps taking his arm—it was a pursuer to shun. It was an inconsistent and ubiquitous fiend too, for, while it was making the whole night behind him dreadful, he darted out into the roadway to avoid dark alleys, fearful of its coming hopping out of them like a dropsical boy’s kite without tail and wings. It hid in doorways too, rubbing its horrible shoulders against doors, and drawing them up to its ears, as if it were laughing. It got into shadows on the road, and lay cunningly on its back to trip him up. All this time it was incessantly hopping on behind and gaining on him, so that when the boy got to his own door he had reason for being half dead. And even then it would not leave him, but followed him upstairs with a bump on every stair, scrambled into bed with him, and bumped down, dead and heavy, on his breast when he fell asleep.
domestication: 他当时是不会停下的，除非是喘口气这样性命攸关的事。这是一场幽灵般的赛跑，早点到头才是上策。他心里笃定那口棺材正在追他，仿佛它竖立着在他身后一蹦一跳，眼看就要追上他，蹦到他身边，说不定还会挽住他的胳膊。这个追踪者是他避之不及的。这玩意儿既矛盾又无处不在，一边让他身后的夜色变得恐怖无比，一边又逼得他冲向马路，生怕它从黑暗的小巷里蹦出来，就像个没尾巴没翅膀的肿腿风筝。它还藏在门洞里，肩膀蹭着门板，仿佛在哈哈大笑。它潜伏在路边的阴影中，狡猾地躺在那儿等着绊倒他。这一路上它一刻不停地跟在他后面蹦跶，离他越来越近，等那孩子跑到自己家门口时，他已经累得半死不活了。可这鬼东西还是不肯放过他，一路跟着他上楼，每上一级台阶就发出“咚咚”声，接着钻进被窝和他一起睡，等他睡着后，就沉甸甸地压在他胸口上。
foreignization: 他那时是不会停下的，除非是比呼吸更必要的事情，因为他跑的是一场幽灵般的赛跑，而且非常渴望能够到达终点。他强烈地感觉到自己看到的棺材正在追赶他；想象着它直立着，窄窄的一端在地上跳动，总是快要追上他，然后跳到他身边——或许还会挽起他的手臂——这是一个必须躲避的追逐者。这还是一个不一致且无处不在的恶魔，因为当它使他身后的整个夜晚变得可怕时，他会冲到马路上，以避开黑暗的小巷，害怕它会像一个没有尾巴和翅膀的浮肿男孩的风筝一样从里面跳出来。它还藏在门廊里，用它可怕的肩膀摩擦门，并把门拉到耳边，仿佛在笑。它钻进路上的阴影中，巧妙地躺在背上，试图绊倒他。所有这段时间，它一直在不断地在他身后跳跃并逐渐靠近他，因此当男孩到达自己的门口时，他已经累得半死。即使到了那时，它也没有放过他，而是跟着他上楼，每走一级楼梯都会发出一声闷响，爬进他的床，当他入睡时，沉重地压在他的胸口。

source: The opened half-door was opened a little further, and secured at that angle for the time. A broad ray of light fell into the garret, and showed the workman with an unfinished shoe upon his lap, pausing in his labour. His few common tools and various scraps of leather were at his feet and on his bench. He had a white beard, raggedly cut, but not very long, a hollow face, and exceedingly bright eyes. The hollowness and thinness of his face would have caused them to look large, under his yet dark eyebrows and his confused white hair, though they had been really otherwise; but, they were naturally large, and looked unnaturally so. His yellow rags of shirt lay open at the throat, and showed his body to be withered and worn. He, and his old canvas frock, and his loose stockings, and all his poor tatters of clothes, had, in a long seclusion from direct light and air, faded down to such a dull uniformity of parchment-yellow, that it would have been hard to say which was which.
domestication: 半掩的门又推开了一些，暂时固定在那个角度。一道宽宽的光线射进阁楼，照见一个工人坐在那里，怀中放着一只未做完的鞋，停下了手里的活儿。他的几件普通工具和零零碎碎的皮革散落在脚边和工作台上。他留着一撮乱糟糟的白胡子，虽然不长，但显得凌乱；脸庞凹陷，眼睛却异常明亮。即使他的眼睛不是真的大，脸上的消瘦和颧骨突出也会让人觉得眼睛很大，何况他本来眼睛就大，看起来更加不同寻常。他那件破旧的黄色衬衣敞开着领口，露出干瘪而疲惫的身体。他身上穿着的老式帆布外套、松垮的袜子，还有所有破烂的衣服，在长期不见阳光和新鲜空气的情况下，都褪成了同样的暗黄色，几乎分不清哪是哪了。

source: “No it doesn’t; I assure you it doesn’t. Having supposed that there was sense where there is no sense, and a laudable ambition where there is not a laudable ambition, I am well out of my mistake, and no harm is done. Young women have committed similar follies often before, and have repented them in poverty and obscurity often before. In an unselfish aspect, I am sorry that the thing is dropped, because it would have been a bad thing for me in a worldly point of view; in a selfish aspect, I am glad that the thing has dropped, because it would have been a bad thing for me in a worldly point of view—it is hardly necessary to say I could have gained nothing by it. There is no harm at all done. I have not proposed to the young lady, and, between ourselves, I am by no means certain, on reflection, that I ever should have committed myself to that extent. Mr. Lorry, you cannot control the mincing vanities and giddinesses of empty-headed girls; you must not expect to do it, or you will always be disappointed. Now, pray say no more about it. I tell you, I regret it on account of others, but I am satisfied on my own account. And I am really very much obliged to you for allowing me to sound you, and for giving me your advice; you know the young lady better than I do; you were right, it never would have done.”
domestication: 不，不会的；我保证不会。我之前误以为无意义之处有深意，没有上进心的地方却认为有雄心壮志，现在认清了错误，也无大碍。年轻姑娘们以前也常犯这样的傻，最后在穷困和默默无闻中后悔不已。从无私的角度讲，我有点儿遗憾事情就这样结束了，因为这对我的前程确实不利；但从自私的角度讲，我又觉得这样挺好的，因为这件事对我的事业确实没什么好处——不用说，我什么也得不到。所以，其实也没什么损失。我还没有向那位小姐求婚呢，说句心里话，经过一番思考，我也不确定自己会不会真的迈出那一步。洛瑞先生，您无法掌控那些空虚女孩的虚荣心和轻浮行为，您也不能期望做到这一点，否则您只会一次次失望。现在，请不要再提这事了。我告诉您，我是为别人感到惋惜，但对自己而言，我很满意。而且，我真的非常感谢您让我有机会跟您谈谈，感谢您的建议；您比我对这位小姐了解得多，您说得对，这事儿确实不合适。

source: A sumptuous man was the Farmer-General. Thirty horses stood in his stables, twenty-four male domestics sat in his halls, six body-women waited on his wife. As one who pretended to do nothing but plunder and forage where he could, the Farmer-General—howsoever his matrimonial relations conduced to social morality—was at least the greatest reality among the personages who attended at the hotel of Monseigneur that day.
domestication: 这农夫总管可真是个阔绰的主儿。他马厩里养了三十匹骏马，大厅里坐着二十四个男仆，连他太太都有六个贴身丫鬟伺候着。这农夫总管，表面上虽说是个四处掠夺、搜刮民脂民膏的主儿，但不管他的婚姻关系对社会道德有多大的贡献，至少在那天来蒙塞尼尔酒店赴宴的宾客中，他是最真实的一个大人物。
foreignization: 农夫总管是一个奢华的人。三十匹马站在他的马厩里，二十四名男仆坐在他的大厅里，六名侍女伺候着他的妻子。作为一个假装什么都不做，只在能抢到东西的地方掠夺和搜刮的人，农夫总管——不管他的婚姻关系如何有助于社会道德——至少是那天在蒙塞尼尔酒店出席的人物中最大的现实。

source: But indeed, at that time, putting to death was a recipe much in vogue with all trades and professions, and not least of all with Tellson’s. Death is Nature’s remedy for all things, and why not Legislation’s? Accordingly, the forger was put to Death; the utterer of a bad note was put to Death; the unlawful opener of a letter was put to Death; the purloiner of forty shillings and sixpence was put to Death; the holder of a horse at Tellson’s door, who made off with it, was put to Death; the coiner of a bad shilling was put to Death; the sounders of three-fourths of the notes in the whole gamut of Crime, were put to Death. Not that it did the least good in the way of prevention—it might almost have been worth remarking that the fact was exactly the reverse—but, it cleared off (as to this world) the trouble of each particular case, and left nothing else connected with it to be looked after. Thus, Tellson’s, in its day, like greater places of business, its contemporaries, had taken so many lives, that, if the heads laid low before it had been ranged on Temple Bar instead of being privately disposed of, they would probably have excluded what little light the ground floor had, in a rather significant manner.
domestication: 但确实，那时处决成了一种流行的手段，各行各业都乐此不疲，尤其是泰尔森银行。俗话说，生死有命，立法何尝不是？于是，伪造者被处死；传递假钞的被处死；非法拆信的被处死；偷了四十先令六便士的被处死；在泰尔森银行门口牵走马匹的被处死；铸造假币的被处死；几乎所有犯罪行为的参与者几乎都难逃一死。这根本起不到预防作用——甚至可以反过来说，一点效果都没有——但这倒是解决了个案问题，再不用操心后续了。就这样，泰尔森银行在那个年代，像其他同辈的大企业一样，处决了不少人。要是那些被砍下的头颅不是秘密处理，而是挂在特emple栅栏上，恐怕连一楼那点微弱的光线都要被遮挡得严严实实，意义深远啊。
foreignization: 但的确，在当时，处决是一种各行各业都非常流行的方法， Tellson’s 也不例外。死亡是自然对一切事物的补救措施，立法为何不能如此？因此，伪造者被处死；流通假币者被处死；非法拆信者被处死；偷窃四十一先令六便士者被处死；在 Tellson’s 门口牵走马匹者被处死；铸造劣质硬币者被处死；几乎所有犯罪行为中的人，无论轻重，都被处死。这并不是说这种做法对预防犯罪丝毫没有好处——几乎可以说事实恰恰相反——但它确实清除了（在这个世界上的）每个具体案件的麻烦，使之不再需要进一步关注。因此，Tellson’s 在其鼎盛时期，像它同时代的更大企业一样，夺去了许多生命，如果那些倒在它脚下的人头不是私下处理而是排列在泰晤士栅栏上，很可能几乎会完全遮挡住一楼的光线，以一种相当意味深长的方式。

source: He accompanied his conductor into a guard-room, smelling of common wine and tobacco, where certain soldiers and patriots, asleep and awake, drunk and sober, and in various neutral states between sleeping and waking, drunkenness and sobriety, were standing and lying about. The light in the guard-house, half derived from the waning oil-lamps of the night, and half from the overcast day, was in a correspondingly uncertain condition. Some registers were lying open on a desk, and an officer of a coarse, dark aspect, presided over these.
domestication: 他跟着向导来到了一个守卫室，屋里弥漫着廉价酒和烟草的味道。一些士兵和爱国人士，有的睡着了，有的醒着，有的醉醺醺的，有的还清醒，处于半梦半醒、似醉非醉的状态，或站或躺。守卫室的光线昏暗不定，一半来自夜间的油灯，另一半则透过阴沉的天空透进来。桌子上摊开着几本登记簿，一位相貌粗犷、面色黝黑的军官正坐在那里管理着这些。
foreignization: 他陪同他的向导进入了一间散发着普通酒和烟草气味的警卫室，那里有一些士兵和爱国者，有的睡着，有的醒着，有的醉了，有的清醒，处于睡眠与清醒、醉酒与清醒之间的各种中性状态，或站或躺。警卫室里的光线，一半来自夜间即将燃尽的油灯，一半来自阴沉的白天，处于相应的不确定状态。桌上放着一些打开的登记册，一位长相粗犷、面色黝黑的军官主持着这些事务。

source: The man slept on, indifferent to showers of hail and intervals of brightness, to sunshine on his face and shadow, to the paltering lumps of dull ice on his body and the diamonds into which the sun changed them, until the sun was low in the west, and the sky was glowing. Then, the mender of roads having got his tools together and all things ready to go down into the village, roused him.
domestication: 那人继续睡着，对冰雹的袭击和阳光的交替，对阳光照在脸上又变成阴影，对身上那些沉闷的冰块以及太阳将它们变成的钻石般闪耀的景象，都毫不在意。直到太阳西斜，天边泛起红光，修路工收拾好工具，准备下山回村时，才把他叫醒。
foreignization: 那人继续睡着，对冰雹的阵雨和间歇的光明，对阳光照在他的脸上和阴影，对落在他身上的沉闷冰块以及太阳将它们变成的钻石都无动于衷，直到太阳西下，天空变得通红。然后，修路工收拾好工具，准备好下山去村里，把他叫醒了。

source: “As I was saying; if Monsieur Manette had not died; if he had suddenly and silently disappeared; if he had been spirited away; if it had not been difficult to guess to what dreadful place, though no art could trace him; if he had an enemy in some compatriot who could exercise a privilege that I in my own time have known the boldest people afraid to speak of in a whisper, across the water there; for instance, the privilege of filling up blank forms for the consignment of any one to the oblivion of a prison for any length of time; if his wife had implored the king, the queen, the court, the clergy, for any tidings of him, and all quite in vain;—then the history of your father would have been the history of this unfortunate gentleman, the Doctor of Beauvais.”
domestication: 正如我刚才所说，如果曼内特先生没有死去，而是突然无声无息地消失了；如果他被神秘地带走了；如果人们虽然能猜到他去了某个可怕的地方，却无法找到任何线索；如果他有一个同乡的敌人，这个敌人拥有一种特权，这种特权在当时连最胆大的人都不敢在水那边低语提及，比如可以随意填写空白表格，将任何人关进监狱，让人间蒸发；如果他的妻子向国王、王后、朝廷、教会恳求过他的消息，但都毫无结果——那么你父亲的故事，就是这位不幸的博韦医生的故事了。
foreignization: 正如我所说；如果曼内特先生没有去世；如果他突然且无声无息地消失了；如果他被带走了；如果很难猜到他被带到了什么可怕的地方，尽管任何手段都无法追踪到他；如果他有一个同胞敌人，这个敌人拥有我在我那个时代见过最勇敢的人也不敢在水那边低声提及的特权，例如，填写空白表格将任何人关押进监狱并使其消失的特权，无论时间多长；如果他的妻子向国王、王后、宫廷和教士恳求有关他的任何消息，但都毫无结果；——那么你父亲的历史就会是这位不幸的先生，博韦的医生的历史。
